skill.name: mdr-745-specialist
skill.description: Specialist guidance for EU MDR 2017/745 compliance, device classification, technical documentation, clinical evidence, UDI, EUDAMED, and post-market surveillance planning.
skill.summary: Guide EU MDR 2017/745 compliance work for medical device teams.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against file context and dismissed as false positives. The flagged terms are regulatory Markdown, placeholder reference text, or filenames in code formatting; no execution, exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Static Matches
low_findings.description: False positive. The flagged weak cryptography matches appear in EU MDR regulatory wording, placeholder documentation, or ordinary text, not cryptographic code or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain descriptive MDR compliance text or placeholders. I found no hashing, encryption calls, credential handling, or use of weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown Backtick Command Matches
low_findings.description: False positive. The backticks mark code fences and filenames in Markdown resource lists; they do not execute Ruby, shell, or other external commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is Markdown formatting only. No executable script, command substitution, or command runner appears on the flagged lines.

low_findings.title: Dismissed C2 Keyword Match
low_findings.description: False positive. The text refers to MDR conformity assessment Module C2, a regulatory route, not command-and-control infrastructure.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section discusses Class IIa conformity assessment. The term C2 is a module label and has no network or malware context.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Network Reconnaissance Match
low_findings.description: False positive. The line discusses Notified Body selection criteria and market access, not scanning, probing, or mapping networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is regulatory business planning. I found no host discovery, port scanning, network tooling, or target enumeration.

content.value_statement: Medical device teams need clear MDR guidance across classification, evidence, and documentation. This skill structures EU MDR 2017/745 work into practical compliance tasks.
actual_capabilities: Guides MDR Annex VIII classification and conformity assessment route selection.
Outlines Annex II and Annex III technical documentation requirements.
Helps plan clinical evidence, clinical evaluation, and PMCF activities.
Structures UDI, EUDAMED, and economic operator compliance work.
Supports post-market surveillance, PSUR, incident reporting, and trend reporting planning.
limitations: Does not replace legal advice or Notified Body decisions.
Does not verify current MDR guidance without external research.
Several referenced helper resources are placeholders or not included.
Cannot certify device compliance or submit regulatory filings.

use_cases.title: Classify a Medical Device
use_cases.description: Map intended purpose, duration, invasiveness, and software role to a defensible MDR classification rationale.

use_cases.title: Prepare Technical Documentation
use_cases.description: Create a structured Annex II and Annex III checklist for a device technical file.

use_cases.title: Plan Clinical Evidence
use_cases.description: Identify evidence gaps, PMCF needs, and clinical evaluation updates for MDR compliance planning.

prompt_templates.title: Start Classification Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for MDR classification support.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me classify a medical device under EU MDR Annex VIII. Ask for the minimum information needed first.

prompt_templates.title: Build Documentation Checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for technical file planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an MDR Annex II and Annex III technical documentation checklist for this device: [describe device].

prompt_templates.title: Assess Clinical Evidence Gaps
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for clinical evidence planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this clinical evidence summary and identify MDR gaps, PMCF needs, and likely Notified Body questions.

prompt_templates.title: Create Compliance Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Develop a prioritized MDR compliance roadmap covering classification, technical documentation, UDI, EUDAMED, PMS, and economic operators.

output_examples.input: Classify a reusable surgical instrument under MDR.
output_examples.output: A concise classification rationale that identifies relevant Annex VIII rules, likely class, assumptions, and evidence needed for confirmation.

output_examples.input: Prepare an MDR technical file readiness review.
output_examples.output: A section-by-section readiness checklist covering device description, labeling, risk management, verification, validation, clinical evidence, and PMS.

output_examples.input: Plan PMS deliverables for a Class IIb device.
output_examples.output: A practical PMS deliverable plan with PMS Plan, PSUR, incident reporting, trend reporting, PMCF links, and update cadence.

best_practices: Provide device purpose, risk profile, software role, and clinical claims before asking for classification guidance.
Ask for assumptions and evidence gaps to be listed separately from conclusions.
Validate final regulatory positions with current guidance and qualified regulatory counsel.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated guidance as a Notified Body decision.
Do not request certification claims without verified device evidence.
Do not omit intended purpose, user population, or clinical claims from MDR prompts.

faq.question: What regulation does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on EU MDR 2017/745 for medical device compliance planning and documentation.

faq.question: Can it decide my final device classification?
faq.answer: It can help build a classification rationale, but final decisions require qualified review and may need Notified Body input.

faq.question: Does it create technical documentation?
faq.answer: It can outline documentation structures, checklists, and gap analyses for Annex II and Annex III.

faq.question: Does it cover clinical evidence?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports clinical evaluation, literature evidence planning, clinical investigation considerations, and PMCF planning.

faq.question: Can it check the latest MDR guidance?
faq.answer: Only if the assistant has current research access. Otherwise, verify outputs against current MDCG and authority guidance.

faq.question: Is this legal or regulatory advice?
faq.answer: No. It is an AI guidance aid and should be reviewed by qualified regulatory professionals.
